mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in housing starts indicates a supply-demand mismatch where capacity constraints outpace consumer spending, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, a decline in real consumer credit availability directly restricts corporate financing and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when RSAFS declines during periods where FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings bases, which mechanically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms a labor market cooling that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100,000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances following the dual-month payroll threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate simultaneously increases signals an overheating economy where supply constraints meet tightening liquidity, ultimately triggering profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample directional accuracy beyond 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, a contraction in real consumer credit amplifies demand destruction by limiting household purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both UNRATE exceeds 5.0% and RSAFS declines month-over-month.
